entities.name: IRON TOXICITY
entities.description: A nutrient disorder associated with high concentrations of iron in soil solutions.

entities.name: IRON
entities.description: A chemical element that causes toxicity in high concentrations in soil solutions.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: A plant nutrient (P) whose deficiency is implicated in the occurrence of iron toxicity and whose application decreases iron uptake.

entities.name: POTASSIUM
entities.description: A plant nutrient (K) whose deficiency is implicated in the occurrence of iron toxicity and whose application decreases iron uptake.

entities.name: CALCIUM
entities.description: A plant nutrient (Ca) whose deficiency is implicated in the occurrence of iron toxicity.

entities.name: MAGNESIUM
entities.description: A plant nutrient (Mg) whose deficiency is implicated in the occurrence of iron toxicity.

entities.name: ZINC
entities.description: A plant nutrient (Zn) whose deficiency is implicated in the occurrence of iron toxicity and whose application decreases iron uptake.

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The crop plant studied for iron toxicity tolerance and production.

entities.name: IVORY COAST
entities.description: The country where field experiments were conducted.

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: The region where promising rice cultivars are available.

entities.name: IRON TOXICITY TOLERANCE
entities.description: The genetic ability of rice cultivars to withstand iron toxicity and maintain production.

entities.name: FIELD EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: Studies conducted in the field to evaluate iron toxicity tolerance of rice cultivars.

entities.name: GLASSHOUSE STUDIES
entities.description: Controlled environment studies conducted to test the role of nutrients in iron toxicity.

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A plant nutrient (N) whose application decreases iron uptake in rice tops.

entities.name: RICE PRODUCTION
entities.description: The yield of rice, which can be improved by genetic tolerance to iron toxicity.
